title: eremogone graminea
intro: Eremogone graminea, also known as Western dwarf sandwort, is a small, annual or biennial plant in the carnation family (Caryophyllaceae). It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, including antiseptic and anti-inflammatory effects. The plant is believed to have several health benefits, including soothing skin irritations and reducing fever, due to its antibacterial and antiviral properties. Eremogone graminea can be prepared as an infusion, tincture, or poultice for its medicinal uses.
